rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label — mandatory (scope: maintainer-block only, not student-facing body).
rules_evaluated.notes: Previous REVISE fired on missing [practitioner direct] label in body prose (line 49 Cherny citation). Compoundium 2026-05-03-research-source-type-labels-maintainer-only.md clarifies scope: source-type labels belong in maintainer-block provenance lists only, not student-facing body. Body citation at line 49 ships in builder voice per §10 — no label required. Maintainer-block Frameworks-riffed-on (lines 117-119) carries [practitioner direct] labels for Klaassen and Huryn entries — correct placement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block on any student-facing file with practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.notes: Previous REVISE: maintainer block lacked a Source verification section. Now present at lines 121-122: '**Source verification — MUST DO before first cohort:**' lists the Cherny video URL, notes the freshness-rule exception decision, and names a fallback framing if the video becomes unavailable. Satisfies all four §11 requirements: (1) URL to verify, (2) freshness re-check decision, (3) named fallback, (4) ship-time discipline surfaced.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
Known correct Claude Code facts (easy to get wrong).
Sessions are the unit of work — 'start a new session in X,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Pseudo-URI + kill-switch for public/private content split.

rules_evaluated.notes: Cherny video (May 2025) sits outside the 6-month window. Freshness-rule exception is explicitly logged in maintainer block (line 67): 'Kept outside the 6-month window by decision; pedagogy match unbeaten by successors.' §2 allows explicit exception with rationale. PASS.
Module file contains no fenced prompt blocks. Prompts live in referenced exercise files (orient-and-introspect.md, fix-tests-first.md, compound-and-close.md). N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.notes: No skill invocations in fenced blocks. Module file uses no [Skill: ...] references. N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism check — every named tool, skill, connector, file, or path must exist and be available.
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
rules_evaluated.notes: M1 does not build verification machinery; the compound-and-close exercise produces CLAUDE.local.md. N/A.

Key Concepts (line 32) explicitly surfaces /context and bounded-window reality. CLAUDE.local.md (auto-load tier) named and distinguished from ./CLAUDE.md (team). Context-loading tier language is correct and appropriate for M1 exposure level.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Use /context to see how much your context window has filled up.
platform_claims_checked.notes: Confirmed correct per compounded/2026-04-26-platform-live-test-beats-docs-for-behavior-claims.md Failure 3 live-test. /context shows context budget by category. Language 'how much your context window has filled up' is accurate.

platform_claims_checked.claim: /context shows how much of your window has filled up. The slice of the repo Claude didn't load is the bounded-window reality.
platform_claims_checked.notes: First sentence accurate. Second sentence is a logical inference, not a claim that /context enumerates unloaded files. No misrepresentation.

platform_claims_checked.claim: ./CLAUDE.local.md sits at the top of the next session, waiting to be read (alongside team ./CLAUDE.md if this repo has one; both concatenate into context).
platform_claims_checked.notes: CLAUDE.local.md is in the auto-load tier per check_platform_and_boundaries.md §7a. 'Concatenate into context' is correct.
